zh.lifeSummary: 當Earl Hugh Ross of Ross於1275年出生在蘇格蘭洛-爾費亞時，他的父親William Ross 2nd Earl of Ross25歲，母親Euphemia de Berkeley18歲。他於1329年11月24日與Margaret de Graham在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1333年登記服兵役。他去世於1333年7月19日在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德貝維其可-朋-特維德享年58歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭希倫德希爾 本 費亞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Earl Hugh Ross of Ross于1275年出生在苏格兰洛-爾費亞时，他的父亲William Ross 2nd Earl of Ross25岁，母亲Euphemia de Berkeley18岁。他于1329年11月24日与Margaret de Graham在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1333年登记服兵役。他去世于1333年7月19日在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德貝維其可-朋-特維德享年58岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰希倫德希爾 本 費亞。

nameMeanings.name: Ross
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

variants.name: Ruskin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Tauba, Uzi.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rosce (see Ross 4) + the diminutive suffix -kin.
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish (Ashkenazic) surname, for example   Raskin  .

variants.name: Ros
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.
Swedish: ornamental name from ros ‘rose’.

variants.name: Rose
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rossell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rusling
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Rocelin (Old French Roscelin, Rocelin, ancient Germanic Ruozelin), a double diminutive of Rozo (see Ross ).

variants.name: Rohs
meanings: German: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Joris.
German: topographic name from Middle High German rœsse ‘kiln’, in particular one used for drying hemp and flax or for burning lime.
English and Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Ross  .

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

en.lifeSummary: When Earl Hugh Ross of Ross was born in 1275, in Fearn, Ross-shire, Scotland, his father, William Ross 2nd Earl of Ross, was 25 and his mother, Euphemia de Berkeley, was 18. He married Margaret de Graham on 24 November 1329, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He registered for military service in 1333. He died on 19 July 1333, in Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58, and was buried in Hill of Fearn, Highland, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Earl Hugh Ross of Ross est né en 1275, à Fearn, Ross-shire, Écosse, son père, William Ross 2nd Earl of Ross, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Euphemia de Berkeley, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Margaret de Graham le 24 novembre 1329, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1333. Il est décédé le 19 juillet 1333, à Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Hill of Fearn, Highland, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Earl Hugh Ross of Ross 1275 in Fearn, Ross-shire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Ross 2nd Earl of Ross 25 und seine Mutter Euphemia de Berkeley 18. Er hat am 24. November 1329 in Scotland Margaret de Graham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er hat sich 1333 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 19. Juli 1333 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Hill of Fearn, Highland, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Earl Hugh Ross of Ross è nato il nel 1275 nel Fearn, Ross-shire, Scotland, il suo padre, William Ross 2nd Earl of Ross, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Euphemia de Berkeley, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Margaret de Graham il 24 novembre 1329, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1333. Morì il 19 luglio 1333, nel Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Hill of Fearn, Highland, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Earl Hugh Ross of Ross(이)가 1275년 스컷랜드 로스-실르 피른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Ross 2nd Earl of Ross(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Euphemia de Berkeley(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1329년 11월 24일 스컷랜드에서 Margaret de Graham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1333년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1333년 7월 19일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 버윅-업-트위드에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 히랜드 힐 프 피른에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Earl Hugh Ross of Ross nasceu em 1275, em Fearn, Ross-shire, Scotland, seu pai, William Ross 2nd Earl of Ross, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Euphemia de Berkeley, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret de Graham em 24 de novembro de 1329, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1333. Ele faleceu em 19 de julho de 1333, em Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Hill of Fearn, Highland, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Scotland, Unit: фирн, росс анд кромерти, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.Hill of Fearn, Highland, Scotland: хилл оф фирн, хаиланд, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.Fearn, Ross and Cromarty, Scotland: фирн, росс анд кромерти, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Earl Hugh Ross of Ross родился в 1275 г. в местности фирн, росс-шир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени William Ross 2nd Earl of Ross было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Euphemia de Berkeley, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret de Graham 24 ноября 1329 г. в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. В 1333 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 19 июля 1333 г. в местности бирвикк-упон-твид, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности хилл оф фирн, хаиланд, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Earl Hugh Ross of Ross nació en 1275, en Fearn, Ross-shire, Scotland, su papá, William Ross 2nd Earl of Ross, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Euphemia de Berkeley, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Margaret de Graham el 24 de noviembre de 1329, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1333. Murió el 19 de julio de 1333, en Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Hill of Fearn, Highland, Escocia, Reino Unido.
